Name: spectral_stability_bounds
Title: Spectral Stability Bounds for Training-Free Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that spectral stability bounds can be used to derive enforceable, strictly bounded-overhead protocols for latency-critical inference in training-free machine learning settings.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works in training-free machine learning, such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation. However, our approach focuses on deriving spectral stability bounds using PAC-Bayes frameworks, which provides a more rigorous and transparent method for optimizing latency-critical inference. Unlike existing works, our proposal explicitly maps proposal boundaries against adjacent literature, isolating one high-impact contribution per submission while maintaining modular validation pathways.
Abstract: We propose a novel approach to training-free machine learning that leverages spectral stability bounds to derive enforceable, strictly bounded-overhead protocols for latency-critical inference. Our method uses PAC-Bayes frameworks to establish a rigorous and transparent bound on the optimization landscape's geometry, ensuring that the proposed protocol is both computationally efficient and theoretically sound. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through a series of experiments on single-GPU setups, showcasing its ability to achieve a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to existing baselines. Our work bridges the gap between foundational optimization theory and practical, scalable inference, offering a reproducible, zero-training path to efficient test-time scaling.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed approach on a range of benchmarks, including WILDS and adversarial ICL benchmarks.
Compare the performance of our approach with existing baselines, including Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation.
Investigate the impact of spectral stability bounds on the optimization landscape's geometry and the resulting inference efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed approach may not be applicable to all types of machine learning models or datasets.
The derivation of spectral stability bounds may require significant computational resources and expertise.
The approach may not be robust to certain types of distributional shifts or noisy contexts.
